arabic: طه
transliteration: Taha
translation: Ta Ha

arabic: مَآ أَنزَلۡنَا عَلَيۡكَ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ لِتَشۡقَىٰٓ
transliteration: Ma anzalna AAalayka alqurana litashqa
translation: We have not sent down the Qur’aan upon you to cause you distress.
notes: What is narrated here in this Qur’aanic Chapter is the part of Moses’ story starting at the point when he is on his way back to Egypt after a prolonged stay at Madyan. We can find the earlier part of his story narrated in Verses 28:7 to 28:29. Anecdotes from Moses’ story can be found also at many other places in the Qur’aan.

arabic: إِلَّا تَذۡكِرَةٗ لِّمَن يَخۡشَىٰ
transliteration: Illa tathkiratan liman yakhsha
translation: It is but a Memorandum for one who fears Allah.
notes: The two signs described in Verses 19 to 22 above were not the only miraculous signs shown to Moses. There were many others – like the splitting of the sea to make way for Moses and his people and to drown Pharaoh and his forces – given to him during his prophethood.

arabic: تَنزِيلٗا مِّمَّنۡ خَلَقَ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ ٱلۡعُلَى
transliteration: Tanzeelan mimman khalaqa alarda waalssamawati alAAula
translation: It is a bestowal from the One Who created the earth and the lofty heavens.
notes: This reply of Moses to Pharaoh is noteworthy. Allah guides everything He has created – including man. But man has been given an additional thing: freedom of choice. Exercising this freedom, most of mankind, unfortunately, reject divine guidance and thus ruin themselves.

arabic: ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ
transliteration: Alrrahmanu AAala alAAarshi istawa
translation: From the Gracious One, firmly established on the throne of absolute authority.
notes: Verses 53 to 56 here do not apparently report what Moses told Pharaoh but are added here parenthetically to remind the readers of the Qur’aan of the divine Handiwork that provides enough signs for man to understand and realize the divine Hand working behind the scenes. And Verse 56 reminds the readers that man haughtily rejected these natural and obvious signs.

arabic: لَهُۥ مَا فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا وَمَا تَحۡتَ ٱلثَّرَىٰ
transliteration: Lahu ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi wama baynahuma wama tahta alththara
translation: Everything – that which is in the heavens, on the earth, in between the two, and underneath the ground – is all His.
notes: The context indicates that this objective pronoun refers to Pharaoh; but it could refer to average man in general.

arabic: وَإِن تَجۡهَرۡ بِٱلۡقَوۡلِ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ وَأَخۡفَى
transliteration: Wain tajhar bialqawli fainnahu yaAAlamu alssirra waakhfa
translation: And if you say anything aloud, mind you, He knows the secret, and the hidden!
notes: I.e., the magicians whom Pharaoh had called. The details, about the arrangements made in calling them, are left out here. The narrative is resumed when Moses and the magicians confronted each other on the day appointed.

arabic: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَۖ لَهُ ٱلۡأَسۡمَآءُ ٱلۡحُسۡنَىٰ
transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwa lahu alasmao alhusna
translation: Allah – there is no god but He; His are the very best attributes.
notes: Allah Almighty explains to us, in the foregoing Verses of this Chapter, the unique importance of the Qur’aan. It is not any ordinary book authored by any human. It is a gift to mankind from none other than the Creator of all things, Himself! And, yet, mankind is, by and large, utterly blind to the grand magnificence of the divine Book. It was this Book that had converted a nondescript Arab people into the most potent force on earth. But, once they lost sight of its importance, the same Arab people are now being mercilessly humiliated at the hands of the tiny nation of Israel.

arabic: وَهَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَىٰٓ
transliteration: Wahal ataka hadeethu moosa
translation: And has Moses’ story come down to you?
notes: The manna and the quails are mentioned in Verse 2:57 also. Refer study note 61 thereunder. Quails are a kind of bird, and, manna, a divinely sent down food item especially for Moses’ people while they were on the move after the Exodus.

arabic: إِذۡ رَءَا نَارٗا فَقَالَ لِأَهۡلِهِ ٱمۡكُثُوٓاْ إِنِّيٓ ءَانَسۡتُ نَارٗا لَّعَلِّيٓ ءَاتِيكُم مِّنۡهَا بِقَبَسٍ أَوۡ أَجِدُ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ هُدٗى
transliteration: Ith raa naran faqala liahlihi omkuthoo innee anastu naran laAAallee ateekum minha biqabasin aw ajidu AAala alnnari hudan
translation: When he saw some fire, he said to his wife escorting him, “Wait! I do indeed see a fire. Maybe, I can bring to you there from a live burning coal or come to know about the right way from someone at the fire.”
notes: What is narrated here in this Qur’aanic Chapter is the part of Moses’ story starting at the point when he is on his way back to Egypt after a prolonged stay at Madyan. We can find the earlier part of his story narrated in Verses 28:7 to 28:29. Anecdotes from Moses’ story can be found also at many other places in the Qur’aan.

arabic: فَلَمَّآ أَتَىٰهَا نُودِيَ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ
transliteration: Falamma ataha noodiya ya moosa
translation: And when he came to it (the fire), he was called out, “O Moses!”
notes: Not much, beyond what is stated in this Chapter, about this character leading Moses’ people astray is revealed in the Qur’aan. The character was obviously influenced by the Satan, the avowed enemy of mankind.

arabic: إِنِّيٓ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكَ فَٱخۡلَعۡ نَعۡلَيۡكَ إِنَّكَ بِٱلۡوَادِ ٱلۡمُقَدَّسِ طُوٗى
transliteration: Innee ana rabbuka faikhlaAA naAAlayka innaka bialwadi almuqaddasi tuwan
translation: “Verily, I am your Lord; so, take off your shoes! You are indeed in the sacred valley of Tuwa.”
notes: Refer Verse 2:51. Allah had given Moses an appointment of 40 nights to give him the Law and Guidance.

arabic: وَأَنَا ٱخۡتَرۡتُكَ فَٱسۡتَمِعۡ لِمَا يُوحَىٰٓ
transliteration: Waana ikhtartuka faistamiAA lima yooha
translation: “And I have chosen you, so then listen to what is revealed.”
notes: This promise could only be that they (Moses’ people) would not worship anyone other than Allah during Moses’ absence.

arabic: إِنَّنِيٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعۡبُدۡنِي وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِذِكۡرِيٓ
transliteration: Innanee ana Allahu la ilaha illa ana faoAAbudnee waaqimi alssalata lithikree
translation: “Indeed, I, Myself, am Allah! There is no god but I. So worship Me and establish regular prayer to remember Me.”
notes: What the Samiri did appears to be this: he melted all the ornaments in a furnace, and then cast the molten mass into a shape resembling the body of a calf.

arabic: إِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ ءَاتِيَةٌ أَكَادُ أُخۡفِيهَا لِتُجۡزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفۡسِۭ بِمَا تَسۡعَىٰ
transliteration: Inna alssaAAata atiyatun akadu okhfeeha litujza kullu nafsin bima tasAAa
translation: “The Hour (Last Day) is indeed coming. I will unravel it anytime so that every soul is recompensed for what it strived.”
notes: It appears the Samiri was afflicted with a disease like leprosy.

arabic: فَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكَ عَنۡهَا مَن لَّا يُؤۡمِنُ بِهَا وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ فَتَرۡدَىٰ
transliteration: Fala yasuddannaka AAanha man la yuminu biha waittabaAAa hawahu fatarda
translation: “So let him not, who does not believe in it (the Hour) and follows his low desires, turn you away from it so that you should perish.”

arabic: وَمَا تِلۡكَ بِيَمِينِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Wama tilka biyameenika ya moosa
translation: “And what is that in your right hand, Moses!”
notes: In other words, the stories narrated in the Qur’aan are all facts that have really happened, and not fictitious.

arabic: قَالَ هِيَ عَصَايَ أَتَوَكَّؤُاْ عَلَيۡهَا وَأَهُشُّ بِهَا عَلَىٰ غَنَمِي وَلِيَ فِيهَا مَـَٔارِبُ أُخۡرَىٰ
transliteration: Qala hiya AAasaya atawakkao AAalayha waahushshu biha AAala ghanamee waliya feeha maaribu okhra
translation: Moses said, “It is my staff. I lean upon it and I beat the leaves with it to make them fall upon my sheep, and I have other uses for it.”
notes: From the account (the Qur’aan) mentioned in the last Verse.

arabic: قَالَ أَلۡقِهَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Qala alqiha ya moosa
translation: Allah said, “Cast it down, O Moses!”
notes: In Verse 23:113, the period so estimated is given as ‘a day or part of a day’. And in Verse 30:55, the estimate is ‘hardly an hour’. Those who had not taken care to follow their lives in accordance with the Qur’aan will thus be deluded to think that they had passed a very short time (hardly an hour to about 10 days) between their death and their resurrection. But those who followed the Qur’aan would know that they had passed a much longer time extending to thousands of years. The varying estimates of sinful people may well correspond to the varying times of their deaths prior to the Day of Resurrection. If a person died on the Last Day, when all worldly life will come to an end, his estimate would be ‘hardly an hour’, while it could be many years till the Day of Resurrection. And for a person dying in the initial period of human history, the estimate could be ten days, whereas the actual period would have been thousands of years.

arabic: فَأَلۡقَىٰهَا فَإِذَا هِيَ حَيَّةٞ تَسۡعَىٰ
transliteration: Faalqaha faitha hiya hayyatun tasAAa
translation: And as Moses cast his staff down, it instantly became a serpent moving!
notes: Please see study notes 482 and 483 on Verse 2.255 in this context.

arabic: قَالَ خُذۡهَا وَلَا تَخَفۡۖ سَنُعِيدُهَا سِيرَتَهَا ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
transliteration: Qala khuthha wala takhaf sanuAAeeduha seerataha aloola
translation: Allah said, “Hold it and be not afraid! We will restore it to its original state.”
notes: In Verse 41:44, it has been explained that since the Prophet chosen was an Arab, the divine Message he was entrusted with had to be in his own tongue, Arabic.

arabic: وَٱضۡمُمۡ يَدَكَ إِلَىٰ جَنَاحِكَ تَخۡرُجۡ بَيۡضَآءَ مِنۡ غَيۡرِ سُوٓءٍ ءَايَةً أُخۡرَىٰ
transliteration: Waodmum yadaka ila janahika takhruj baydaa min ghayri sooin ayatan okhra
translation: “And, another sign: put your hand under your arm, it shall come out perfectly white!”
notes: It may be that the Prophet (peace on him) used to get anxious that he should not forget what was revealed to him, and would start reciting the Verse/s before the revelation was complete; hence, this divine admonition. Elsewhere in the Qur’aan (Verses 75:16 to 75:19), we are informed that Allah assured him of His help in making him remember. But this admonition has significance for us too. Allah tells us here that we should not read the Qur’aan hurriedly, but deliberate on what we read and try our best to understand it.

arabic: لِنُرِيَكَ مِنۡ ءَايَٰتِنَا ٱلۡكُبۡرَى
transliteration: Linuriyaka min ayatina alkubra
translation: “That We may show you some of Our great signs.”
notes: The two signs described in Verses 19 to 22 above were not the only miraculous signs shown to Moses. There were many others – like the splitting of the sea to make way for Moses and his people and to drown Pharaoh and his forces – given to him during his prophethood.

arabic: ٱذۡهَبۡ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
transliteration: Ithhab ila firAAawna innahu tagha
translation: “Go to Pharaoh! He has indeed exceeded all bounds.”
notes: The divine words of this Verse are exactly like a part of Verse 2:34. Please see study notes 27 to 29 on that Verse of Chapter 2.

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱشۡرَحۡ لِي صَدۡرِي
transliteration: Qala rabbi ishrah lee sadree
translation: He said, “O my Lord! Broaden my mind for me.”
notes: The Lord chose Adam to fulfil His word to the angels that He would send His vicegerent to the earth [Verse 2:30].

arabic: وَيَسِّرۡ لِيٓ أَمۡرِي
transliteration: Wayassir lee amree
translation: “And make my task easy for me.” ”

arabic: وَٱحۡلُلۡ عُقۡدَةٗ مِّن لِّسَانِي
transliteration: Waohlul AAuqdatan min lisanee
translation: “And correct the stammer in my speech. 20: 28. “Make them understand what I say.”
notes:
Life of a person who fails to abide by the divine instructions given in the Qur’aan would be straitened, miserable, ignominious, humiliating, or otherwise unsatisfactory. He/she is bound to have a distressing feeling of unfulfilment, guilt and failure at the end of it. And it is a sad reflection on Muslims – on an overwhelming majority of them at the least – that they have woefully taken no heed of this divine mantra, enunciated in these two Verses 123 and 124, for leading a good, satisfactory life in this world.

The backdrop for this Verse is apparent from the context. The polytheists in Makkah would not embrace Islam despite the Prophet’s best efforts. And, in the divine scheme of things, those polytheists were not scheduled to be punished as yet. So they continued belittling the Prophet’s Mission. It was in these circumstances that Allah advises him to have patience and to seek his inner peace of mind by glorifying Allah continually, day and night.

arabic: يَفۡقَهُواْ قَوۡلِي
transliteration: Yafqahoo qawlee
translation: That they may understand my speech.

arabic: وَٱجۡعَل لِّي وَزِيرٗا مِّنۡ أَهۡلِي
transliteration: WaijAAal lee wazeeran min ahlee
translation: “And appoint, from my family, an assistant for me.”
notes: But it would not be proper to interpret this Verse – as many commentators do – as prescribing the times for the ritual prayers. The Qur’aan, the Creator’s Own Manual for guidance of mankind, could not have been so vague in the timings. The timings are exactly prescribed in Verses 17:78, 11:114 and 2:238.

arabic: هَٰرُونَ أَخِي
transliteration: Haroona akhee
translation: “My brother, Aaron.”
notes: The Qur’aan reiterates and further explains and clarifies what the earlier divine scriptures mention or stipulate. This by itself is a clear sign from the Lord that the unlettered Muhammad (peace on him) was indeed a Messenger from Allah. How else could he know the accurate details?

arabic: ٱشۡدُدۡ بِهِۦٓ أَزۡرِي
transliteration: Oshdud bihi azree
translation: “Strengthen me with his support.”

arabic: وَأَشۡرِكۡهُ فِيٓ أَمۡرِي
transliteration: Waashrikhu fee amree
translation: “And make him my associate in my work.”

arabic: كَيۡ نُسَبِّحَكَ كَثِيرٗا
transliteration: Kay nusabbihaka katheeran
translation: “So that we should glorify You much.”

arabic: وَنَذۡكُرَكَ كَثِيرًا
transliteration: Wanathkuraka katheeran
translation: “And remember You often.”

arabic: إِنَّكَ كُنتَ بِنَا بَصِيرٗا
transliteration: Innaka kunta bina baseeran
translation: “You do indeed see us.”

arabic: قَالَ قَدۡ أُوتِيتَ سُؤۡلَكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Qala qad ooteeta sulaka ya moosa
translation: Allah said, “Your petition stands granted, O Moses!”

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ مَنَنَّا عَلَيۡكَ مَرَّةً أُخۡرَىٰٓ
transliteration: Walaqad mananna AAalayka marratan okhra
translation: "And We did confer a favour on thee another time.”

arabic: إِذۡ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ
transliteration: Ith awhayna ila ommika ma yooha
translation: “When We revealed to your mother that which was revealed.”

arabic: أَنِ ٱقۡذِفِيهِ فِي ٱلتَّابُوتِ فَٱقۡذِفِيهِ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ فَلۡيُلۡقِهِ ٱلۡيَمُّ بِٱلسَّاحِلِ يَأۡخُذۡهُ عَدُوّٞ لِّي وَعَدُوّٞ لَّهُۥۚ وَأَلۡقَيۡتُ عَلَيۡكَ مَحَبَّةٗ مِّنِّي وَلِتُصۡنَعَ عَلَىٰ عَيۡنِيٓ
transliteration: Ani iqthifeehi fee alttabooti faiqthifeehi fee alyammi falyulqihi alyammu bialssahili yakhuthhu AAaduwwun lee waAAaduwwun lahu waalqaytu AAalayka mahabbatan minnee walitusnaAAa AAala AAaynee
translation: “That she should put him into a chest, and then put the chest onto the river. Then the river should throw him on the shore. There, one, who is an enemy to Me and enemy to him, should pick him up. And I made you lovable so that you might be brought up under My watchful eye.”

arabic: إِذۡ تَمۡشِيٓ أُخۡتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكُمۡ عَلَىٰ مَن يَكۡفُلُهُۥۖ فَرَجَعۡنَٰكَ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ كَيۡ تَقَرَّ عَيۡنُهَا وَلَا تَحۡزَنَۚ وَقَتَلۡتَ نَفۡسٗا فَنَجَّيۡنَٰكَ مِنَ ٱلۡغَمِّ وَفَتَنَّـٰكَ فُتُونٗاۚ فَلَبِثۡتَ سِنِينَ فِيٓ أَهۡلِ مَدۡيَنَ ثُمَّ جِئۡتَ عَلَىٰ قَدَرٖ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Ith tamshee okhtuka fataqoolu hal adullukum AAala man yakfuluhu farajaAAnaka ila ommika kay taqarra AAaynuha wala tahzana waqatalta nafsan fanajjaynaka mina alghammi wafatannaka futoonan falabithta sineena fee ahli madyana thumma jita AAala qadarin ya moosa
translation: “When your sister went and said, ‘May I lead you to one who will feed him?’ Thus We returned you to your mother so that she should be comforted and not grieved. And you killed a man, and then We delivered you from that worry. And We tried you variously. Then you stayed for years among the people of Madyan. Then you came here as ordained, O Moses!”

arabic: وَٱصۡطَنَعۡتُكَ لِنَفۡسِي
transliteration: WaistanaAAtuka linafsee
translation: “And I have made you for Myself.”

arabic: ٱذۡهَبۡ أَنتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِـَٔايَٰتِي وَلَا تَنِيَا فِي ذِكۡرِي
transliteration: Ithhab anta waakhooka biayatee wala taniya fee thikree
translation: “Now go, you and your brother, with My signs and be not remiss in remembering Me!”

arabic: ٱذۡهَبَآ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
transliteration: Ithhaba ila firAAawna innahu tagha
translation: “Go both of you to Pharaoh! He has indeed committed transgression.”

arabic: فَقُولَا لَهُۥ قَوۡلٗا لَّيِّنٗا لَّعَلَّهُۥ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوۡ يَخۡشَىٰ
transliteration: Faqoola lahu qawlan layyinan laAAallahu yatathakkaru aw yakhsha
translation: “And speak to him gently. Maybe he takes heed or refrains from further evil.”

arabic: قَالَا رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا نَخَافُ أَن يَفۡرُطَ عَلَيۡنَآ أَوۡ أَن يَطۡغَىٰ
transliteration: Qala rabbana innana nakhafu an yafruta AAalayna aw an yatgha
translation: Both said, “O our Lord! We do indeed fear that he may take no heed of us or that he may commit some transgression.”

arabic: قَالَ لَا تَخَافَآۖ إِنَّنِي مَعَكُمَآ أَسۡمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ
transliteration: Qala la takhafa innanee maAAakuma asmaAAu waara
translation: Allah said, “Fear not! I, Myself, am indeed with you both; I hear, and I see.”

arabic: فَأۡتِيَاهُ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولَا رَبِّكَ فَأَرۡسِلۡ مَعَنَا بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ وَلَا تُعَذِّبۡهُمۡۖ قَدۡ جِئۡنَٰكَ بِـَٔايَةٖ مِّن رَّبِّكَۖ وَٱلسَّلَٰمُ عَلَىٰ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلۡهُدَىٰٓ
transliteration: Fatiyahu faqoola inna rasoola rabbika faarsil maAAana banee israeela wala tuAAaththibhum qad jinaka biayatin min rabbika waalssalamu AAala mani ittabaAAa alhuda
translation: “So go you both to him and say, ‘We two are, indeed, Messengers of your Lord. Send the Children of Israel with us and torment them not! Verily, we have brought to you a sign from your Lord. And peace on him who follows the guidance!’

arabic: إِنَّا قَدۡ أُوحِيَ إِلَيۡنَآ أَنَّ ٱلۡعَذَابَ عَلَىٰ مَن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ
transliteration: Inna qad oohiya ilayna anna alAAathaba AAala man kaththaba watawalla
translation: ‘It has indeed been revealed to us that punishment will come upon him who rejects and turns away.’”

arabic: قَالَ فَمَن رَّبُّكُمَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Qala faman rabbukuma ya moosa
translation: Pharaoh asked, “And who is the Lord of you two, O Moses?”

arabic: قَالَ رَبُّنَا ٱلَّذِيٓ أَعۡطَىٰ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ خَلۡقَهُۥ ثُمَّ هَدَىٰ
transliteration: Qala rabbuna allathee aAAta kulla shayin khalqahu thumma hada
translation: Moses said, “Our Lord is He Who gave everything its creation, and then guided it.”
notes: This reply of Moses to Pharaoh is noteworthy. Allah guides everything He has created – including man. But man has been given an additional thing: freedom of choice. Exercising this freedom, most of mankind, unfortunately, reject divine guidance and thus ruin themselves.

arabic: قَالَ فَمَا بَالُ ٱلۡقُرُونِ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
transliteration: Qala fama balu alqurooni aloola
translation: Pharaoh asked, “Then what about the earlier generations?”

arabic: قَالَ عِلۡمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّي فِي كِتَٰبٖۖ لَّا يَضِلُّ رَبِّي وَلَا يَنسَى
transliteration: Qala AAilmuha AAinda rabbee fee kitabin la yadillu rabbee wala yansa
translation: Moses said, “The knowledge thereof is in a record with my Lord. My Lord errs not, nor does He forget.”

arabic: ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَهۡدٗا وَسَلَكَ لَكُمۡ فِيهَا سُبُلٗا وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ فَأَخۡرَجۡنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٗا مِّن نَّبَاتٖ شَتَّىٰ
transliteration: Allathee jaAAala lakumu alarda mahdan wasalaka lakum feeha subulan waanzala mina alssamai maan faakhrajna bihi azwajan min nabatin shatta
translation: The Lord Who made the earth for you a flattened expanse and laid for you therein paths. And He sent down water from the sky. Then thereby We have brought out diverse herbs/plants in pairs.
notes: Verses 53 to 56 here do not apparently report what Moses told Pharaoh but are added here parenthetically to remind the readers of the Qur’aan of the divine Handiwork that provides enough signs for man to understand and realize the divine Hand working behind the scenes. And Verse 56 reminds the readers that man haughtily rejected these natural and obvious signs.

arabic: كُلُواْ وَٱرۡعَوۡاْ أَنۡعَٰمَكُمۡۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ لِّأُوْلِي ٱلنُّهَىٰ
transliteration: Kuloo wairAAaw anAAamakum inna fee thalika laayatin liolee alnnuha
translation: Eat and tend your cattle. There are indeed signs in that for those who are prudent.

arabic: ۞مِنۡهَا خَلَقۡنَٰكُمۡ وَفِيهَا نُعِيدُكُمۡ وَمِنۡهَا نُخۡرِجُكُمۡ تَارَةً أُخۡرَىٰ
transliteration: Minha khalaqnakum wafeeha nuAAeedukum waminha nukhrijukum taratan okhra
translation: From it (earth) We created you and into it We shall send you back and from it will We raise you a second time.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَرَيۡنَٰهُ ءَايَٰتِنَا كُلَّهَا فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبَىٰ
transliteration: Walaqad araynahu ayatina kullaha fakaththaba waaba
translation: And verily We showed him Our signs, all of them, but he rejected and turned arrogant.
notes: The context indicates that this objective pronoun refers to Pharaoh; but it could refer to average man in general.

arabic: قَالَ أَجِئۡتَنَا لِتُخۡرِجَنَا مِنۡ أَرۡضِنَا بِسِحۡرِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Qala ajitana litukhrijana min ardina bisihrika ya moosa
translation: Pharaoh said, “Have you come to drive us out of our land by your magic, O Moses?”

arabic: فَلَنَأۡتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحۡرٖ مِّثۡلِهِۦ فَٱجۡعَلۡ بَيۡنَنَا وَبَيۡنَكَ مَوۡعِدٗا لَّا نُخۡلِفُهُۥ نَحۡنُ وَلَآ أَنتَ مَكَانٗا سُوٗى
transliteration: Falanatiyannaka bisihrin mithlihi faijAAal baynana wabaynaka mawAAidan la nukhlifuhu nahnu wala anta makanan suwan
translation: “We shall certainly then bring to you similar magic. Let us then, between us and you, have an appointment at a mutually convenient place, which we should neither of us fail to keep.”

arabic: قَالَ مَوۡعِدُكُمۡ يَوۡمُ ٱلزِّينَةِ وَأَن يُحۡشَرَ ٱلنَّاسُ ضُحٗى
transliteration: Qala mawAAidukum yawmu alzzeenati waan yuhshara alnnasu duhan
translation: Moses said, “Let your appointment be on the day of the Festival and let the people be gathered together in the morning.”

arabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فَجَمَعَ كَيۡدَهُۥ ثُمَّ أَتَىٰ
transliteration: Fatawalla firAAawnu fajamaAAa kaydahu thumma ata
translation: So Pharaoh withdrew, devised his plan of action, and then came.

arabic: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ وَيۡلَكُمۡ لَا تَفۡتَرُواْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبٗا فَيُسۡحِتَكُم بِعَذَابٖۖ وَقَدۡ خَابَ مَنِ ٱفۡتَرَىٰ
transliteration: Qala lahum moosa waylakum la taftaroo AAala Allahi kathiban fayushitakum biAAathabin waqad khaba mani iftara
translation: Moses said to them , “Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, lest He should destroy you by a punishment. And he, who fabricates, does indeed fail.”
notes: I.e., the magicians whom Pharaoh had called. The details, about the arrangements made in calling them, are left out here. The narrative is resumed when Moses and the magicians confronted each other on the day appointed.

arabic: فَتَنَٰزَعُوٓاْ أَمۡرَهُم بَيۡنَهُمۡ وَأَسَرُّواْ ٱلنَّجۡوَىٰ
transliteration: FatanazaAAoo amrahum baynahum waasarroo alnnajwa
translation: And the magicians debated among themselves their response to Moses, but kept the discourse secret.

arabic: قَالُوٓاْ إِنۡ هَٰذَٰنِ لَسَٰحِرَٰنِ يُرِيدَانِ أَن يُخۡرِجَاكُم مِّنۡ أَرۡضِكُم بِسِحۡرِهِمَا وَيَذۡهَبَا بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ ٱلۡمُثۡلَىٰ
transliteration: Qaloo in hathani lasahirani yureedani an yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihima wayathhaba bitareeqatikumu almuthla
translation: They said (among themselves), “Indeed, these two are just magicians. They wish to turn you out of your land by their magic and to deprive you of your best traditions.”

arabic: فَأَجۡمِعُواْ كَيۡدَكُمۡ ثُمَّ ٱئۡتُواْ صَفّٗاۚ وَقَدۡ أَفۡلَحَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ مَنِ ٱسۡتَعۡلَىٰ
transliteration: FaajmiAAoo kaydakum thumma itoo saffan waqad aflaha alyawma mani istaAAla
translation: “So do combine your plan, and then come in line. And he that is superior will certainly win this day.”

arabic: قَالُواْ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلۡقِيَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنۡ أَلۡقَىٰ
transliteration: Qaloo ya moosa imma an tulqiya waimma an nakoona awwala man alqa
translation: They said (aloud), “O Moses! Will you do your act, or shall we be the first to do it?”

arabic: قَالَ بَلۡ أَلۡقُواْۖ فَإِذَا حِبَالُهُمۡ وَعِصِيُّهُمۡ يُخَيَّلُ إِلَيۡهِ مِن سِحۡرِهِمۡ أَنَّهَا تَسۡعَىٰ
transliteration: Qala bal alqoo faitha hibaluhum waAAisiyyuhum yukhayyalu ilayhi min sihrihim annaha tasAAa
translation: He said, “No! You do it.” And lo! Their ropes and their sticks appeared to him (Moses), by their magic, as if they were moving.

arabic: فَأَوۡجَسَ فِي نَفۡسِهِۦ خِيفَةٗ مُّوسَىٰ
transliteration: Faawjasa fee nafsihi kheefatan moosa
translation: So Moses felt a fear rising within him.

arabic: قُلۡنَا لَا تَخَفۡ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰ
transliteration: Qulna la takhaf innaka anta alaAAla
translation: WE said, “Fear not, you shall indeed have the upper hand.”

arabic: وَأَلۡقِ مَا فِي يَمِينِكَ تَلۡقَفۡ مَا صَنَعُوٓاْۖ إِنَّمَا صَنَعُواْ كَيۡدُ سَٰحِرٖۖ وَلَا يُفۡلِحُ ٱلسَّاحِرُ حَيۡثُ أَتَىٰ
transliteration: Waalqi ma fee yameenika talqaf ma sanaAAoo innama sanaAAoo kaydu sahirin wala yuflihu alssahiru haythu ata
translation: “And cast down that which is in your right hand. It shall devour what they have contrived. They have contrived only what a magician could plan. And the magician shall not succeed wherever he may have come from.”

arabic: فَأُلۡقِيَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدٗا قَالُوٓاْ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Faolqiya alssaharatu sujjadan qaloo amanna birabbi haroona wamoosa
translation: And the magicians were obliged to fall prostrate! They said, “We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.”

arabic: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ قَبۡلَ أَنۡ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمۡۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِي عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحۡرَۖ فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيۡدِيَكُمۡ وَأَرۡجُلَكُم مِّنۡ خِلَٰفٖ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمۡ فِي جُذُوعِ ٱلنَّخۡلِ وَلَتَعۡلَمُنَّ أَيُّنَآ أَشَدُّ عَذَابٗا وَأَبۡقَىٰ
transliteration: Qala amantum lahu qabla an athana lakum innahu lakabeerukumu allathee AAallamakumu alssihra falaoqattiAAanna aydiyakum waarjulakum min khilafin walaosallibannakum fee juthooAAi alnnakhli walataAAlamunna ayyuna ashaddu AAathaban waabqa
translation: Pharaoh said, “You believed in him before I gave you leave. He ought to have been your superior who taught you magic. I will then certainly cut off your hands and your feet from opposite sides, and I will certainly crucify you on the trunks of the palm trees. And certainly you will come to know which of us is the more severe and the more persistent in punishing.”

arabic: قَالُواْ لَن نُّؤۡثِرَكَ عَلَىٰ مَا جَآءَنَا مِنَ ٱلۡبَيِّنَٰتِ وَٱلَّذِي فَطَرَنَاۖ فَٱقۡضِ مَآ أَنتَ قَاضٍۖ إِنَّمَا تَقۡضِي هَٰذِهِ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنۡيَآ
transliteration: Qaloo lan nuthiraka AAala ma jaana mina albayyinati waallathee fatarana faiqdi ma anta qadin innama taqdee hathihi alhayata alddunya
translation: The magicians said, “There is no question of our preferring you over Him Who made us, after what has come to us of clear signs. So, decide whatever you want to. You can only decide about life in this world.”

arabic: إِنَّآ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّنَا لِيَغۡفِرَ لَنَا خَطَٰيَٰنَا وَمَآ أَكۡرَهۡتَنَا عَلَيۡهِ مِنَ ٱلسِّحۡرِۗ وَٱللَّهُ خَيۡرٞ وَأَبۡقَىٰٓ
transliteration: Inna amanna birabbina liyaghfira lana khatayana wama akrahtana AAalayhi mina alssihri waAllahu khayrun waabqa
translation: “We do indeed believe in our Lord. May He forgive us our sins and that magic which you forced upon us. And Allah is better and everlasting.”

arabic: إِنَّهُۥ مَن يَأۡتِ رَبَّهُۥ مُجۡرِمٗا فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحۡيَىٰ
transliteration: Innahu man yati rabbahu mujriman fainna lahu jahannama la yamootu feeha wala yahya
translation: Whoever comes to his Lord as a sinner, for him indeed is Hell. He shall not die therein, nor shall he live.

arabic: وَمَن يَأۡتِهِۦ مُؤۡمِنٗا قَدۡ عَمِلَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ فَأُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلدَّرَجَٰتُ ٱلۡعُلَىٰ
transliteration: Waman yatihi muminan qad AAamila alssalihati faolaika lahumu alddarajatu alAAula
translation: And whoever comes to Him as a believer with righteous deeds to his credit, he shall be among those who shall enjoy high ranks.

arabic: جَنَّـٰتُ عَدۡنٖ تَجۡرِي مِن تَحۡتِهَا ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَاۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ
transliteration: Jannatu AAadnin tajree min tahtiha alanharu khalideena feeha wathalika jazao man tazakka
translation: Gardens of perpetuity, beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein. And that is the reward of one who purifies oneself.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنۡ أَسۡرِ بِعِبَادِي فَٱضۡرِبۡ لَهُمۡ طَرِيقٗا فِي ٱلۡبَحۡرِ يَبَسٗا لَّا تَخَٰفُ دَرَكٗا وَلَا تَخۡشَىٰ
transliteration: Walaqad awhayna ila moosa an asri biAAibadee faidrib lahum tareeqan fee albahri yabasan la takhafu darakan wala takhsha
translation: And We did reveal to Moses that he should “travel by night with My devotees, then make for them a dry path in the sea, without any fear of being overtaken or of any other sort.”

arabic: فَأَتۡبَعَهُمۡ فِرۡعَوۡنُ بِجُنُودِهِۦ فَغَشِيَهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡيَمِّ مَا غَشِيَهُمۡ
transliteration: FaatbaAAahum firAAawnu bijunoodihi faghashiyahum mina alyammi ma ghashiyahum
translation: And Pharaoh followed them with his armies. And so there came upon them from the sea that which came upon them.
notes: They all died by drowning and got annihilated therein – except for Pharaoh’s dead body which got divinely preserved to serve as a sign for generations to come (refer Verse 10:92). His body was later discovered, with a thick layer of salt theron, preserved as a mummy in one of the pyramids. The discovery was made in 1881, over 12 centuries after the revelation of the Qur’aan. At that time no man knew how and where the dead body was preserved. Verse 10:92 therefore provides a clear sign that the Qur’aan is indeed a divine Message. But, as the Verse itself notes, most of mankind remain blind to such signs.

arabic: وَأَضَلَّ فِرۡعَوۡنُ قَوۡمَهُۥ وَمَا هَدَىٰ
transliteration: Waadalla firAAawnu qawmahu wama hada
translation: And Pharaoh led his people astray and guided them not.

arabic: يَٰبَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ قَدۡ أَنجَيۡنَٰكُم مِّنۡ عَدُوِّكُمۡ وَوَٰعَدۡنَٰكُمۡ جَانِبَ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلۡأَيۡمَنَ وَنَزَّلۡنَا عَلَيۡكُمُ ٱلۡمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلۡوَىٰ
transliteration: Ya banee israeela qad anjaynakum min AAaduwwikum wawaAAadnakum janiba alttoori alaymana wanazzalna AAalaykumu almanna waalssalwa
translation: O Children of Israel! We did verily deliver you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the right side of the mountain, and We sent to you the manna and the quails.
notes: The manna and the quails are mentioned in Verse 2:57 also. Refer study note 61 thereunder. Quails are a kind of bird, and, manna, a divinely sent down food item especially for Moses’ people while they were on the move after the Exodus.

arabic: كُلُواْ مِن طَيِّبَٰتِ مَا رَزَقۡنَٰكُمۡ وَلَا تَطۡغَوۡاْ فِيهِ فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيۡكُمۡ غَضَبِيۖ وَمَن يَحۡلِلۡ عَلَيۡهِ غَضَبِي فَقَدۡ هَوَىٰ
transliteration: Kuloo min tayyibati ma razaqnakum wala tatghaw feehi fayahilla AAalaykum ghadabee waman yahlil AAalayhi ghadabee faqad hawa
translation: Make use of the good things We have provided you with and be not inordinate therein lest My wrath should become due upon you. And he, upon whom My wrath becomes due, certainly perishes.

arabic: وَإِنِّي لَغَفَّارٞ لِّمَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحٗا ثُمَّ ٱهۡتَدَىٰ
transliteration: Wainnee laghaffarun liman taba waamana waAAamila salihan thumma ihtada
translation: And I am indeed most forgiving to him who repents and believes and does good deeds, and then follows the right direction.

arabic: ۞وَمَآ أَعۡجَلَكَ عَن قَوۡمِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Wama aAAjalaka AAan qawmika ya moosa
translation: “And what made you come here earlier than your people, O Moses?”
notes: Refer Verse 2:51 in this context. The Verse here (20:83) indicates that Moses was called along with his people for this appointment, but Moses came ahead of his people leaving them behind closeby.

arabic: قَالَ هُمۡ أُوْلَآءِ عَلَىٰٓ أَثَرِي وَعَجِلۡتُ إِلَيۡكَ رَبِّ لِتَرۡضَىٰ
transliteration: Qala hum olai AAala atharee waAAajiltu ilayka rabbi litarda
translation: Moses said, “They are close behind me. And I hastened to You, my Lord, that You might be pleased.”

arabic: قَالَ فَإِنَّا قَدۡ فَتَنَّا قَوۡمَكَ مِنۢ بَعۡدِكَ وَأَضَلَّهُمُ ٱلسَّامِرِيُّ
transliteration: Qala fainna qad fatanna qawmaka min baAAdika waadallahumu alssamiriyyu
translation: Allah said, “But then We put your people on trial after you had left them behind, and the Samiri has led them astray.”
notes: Not much, beyond what is stated in this Chapter, about this character leading Moses’ people astray is revealed in the Qur’aan. The character was obviously influenced by the Satan, the avowed enemy of mankind.

arabic: فَرَجَعَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ قَوۡمِهِۦ غَضۡبَٰنَ أَسِفٗاۚ قَالَ يَٰقَوۡمِ أَلَمۡ يَعِدۡكُمۡ رَبُّكُمۡ وَعۡدًا حَسَنًاۚ أَفَطَالَ عَلَيۡكُمُ ٱلۡعَهۡدُ أَمۡ أَرَدتُّمۡ أَن يَحِلَّ عَلَيۡكُمۡ غَضَبٞ مِّن رَّبِّكُمۡ فَأَخۡلَفۡتُم مَّوۡعِدِي
transliteration: FarajaAAa moosa ila qawmihi ghadbana asifan qala ya qawmi alam yaAAidkum rabbukum waAAdan hasanan afatala AAalaykumu alAAahdu am aradtum an yahilla AAalaykum ghadabun min rabbikum faakhlaftum mawAAidee
translation: Then Moses returned to his people, angry and sorrowful. He Said, “O my people! Did not your Lord give you a good promise? Did then the time for fulfillment of the promise seem too long to you? Or did you wish to incur wrath from your Lord, so you broke your promise to me?
notes:
Refer Verse 2:51. Allah had given Moses an appointment of 40 nights to give him the Law and Guidance.

This promise could only be that they (Moses’ people) would not worship anyone other than Allah during Moses’ absence.

arabic: قَالُواْ مَآ أَخۡلَفۡنَا مَوۡعِدَكَ بِمَلۡكِنَا وَلَٰكِنَّا حُمِّلۡنَآ أَوۡزَارٗا مِّن زِينَةِ ٱلۡقَوۡمِ فَقَذَفۡنَٰهَا فَكَذَٰلِكَ أَلۡقَى ٱلسَّامِرِيُّ
transliteration: Qaloo ma akhlafna mawAAidaka bimalkina walakinna hummilna awzaran min zeenati alqawmi faqathafnaha fakathalika alqa alssamiriyyu
translation: They said, “We did not break our promise to you of our own accord, but we were made to bear the burden of the ornaments of the people. So, we got rid of that burden just as the Samiri did.”

arabic: فَأَخۡرَجَ لَهُمۡ عِجۡلٗا جَسَدٗا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٞ فَقَالُواْ هَٰذَآ إِلَٰهُكُمۡ وَإِلَٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِيَ
transliteration: Faakhraja lahum AAijlan jasadan lahu khuwarun faqaloo hatha ilahukum wailahu moosa fanasiya
translation: Then he (Samiri) brought out for them a body of a calf , from which emanated a low sound. Then he told them, “This is your god and the god of Moses. But he (Moses) has forgotten it.”
notes: What the Samiri did appears to be this: he melted all the ornaments in a furnace, and then cast the molten mass into a shape resembling the body of a calf.

arabic: أَفَلَا يَرَوۡنَ أَلَّا يَرۡجِعُ إِلَيۡهِمۡ قَوۡلٗا وَلَا يَمۡلِكُ لَهُمۡ ضَرّٗا وَلَا نَفۡعٗا
transliteration: Afala yarawna alla yarjiAAu ilayhim qawlan wala yamliku lahum darran wala nafAAan
translation: Could they not see that it did not respond to them at all, and that it did have no power to do any harm or to bring any benefit to them?

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ قَالَ لَهُمۡ هَٰرُونُ مِن قَبۡلُ يَٰقَوۡمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِي وَأَطِيعُوٓاْ أَمۡرِي
transliteration: Walaqad qala lahum haroonu min qablu ya qawmi innama futintum bihi wainna rabbakumu alrrahmanu faittabiAAoonee waateeAAoo amree
translation: And certainly had Aaron told them earlier, “O my people! You are only being tried therewith. And, indeed, your Lord is the Gracious One. Follow me then and obey my order!”

arabic: قَالُواْ لَن نَّبۡرَحَ عَلَيۡهِ عَٰكِفِينَ حَتَّىٰ يَرۡجِعَ إِلَيۡنَا مُوسَىٰ
transliteration: Qaloo lan nabraha AAalayhi AAakifeena hatta yarjiAAa ilayna moosa
translation: They had said, “We will not abandon worshipping it until Moses returns to us.”

arabic: قَالَ يَٰهَٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذۡ رَأَيۡتَهُمۡ ضَلُّوٓاْ
transliteration: Qala ya haroonu ma manaAAaka ith raaytahum dalloo
translation: Moses said, “O Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,

arabic: أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِۖ أَفَعَصَيۡتَ أَمۡرِي
transliteration: Alla tattabiAAani afaAAasayta amree
translation: From following me? Did you then go against my instruction?”

arabic: قَالَ يَبۡنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأۡخُذۡ بِلِحۡيَتِي وَلَا بِرَأۡسِيٓۖ إِنِّي خَشِيتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقۡتَ بَيۡنَ بَنِيٓ إِسۡرَـٰٓءِيلَ وَلَمۡ تَرۡقُبۡ قَوۡلِي
transliteration: Qala ya bna omma la takhuth bilihyatee wala birasee innee khasheetu an taqoola farraqta bayna banee israeela walam tarqub qawlee
translation: Aaron said, “O son of my mother! Seize me not by my beard and by my head. I was indeed afraid you might say, ‘You have caused a rift among the children of Israel and not waited for my word.’”

arabic: قَالَ فَمَا خَطۡبُكَ يَٰسَٰمِرِيُّ
transliteration: Qala fama khatbuka ya samiriyyu
translation: Moses then asked, “What have you to say on this, O Samiri?”

arabic: قَالَ بَصُرۡتُ بِمَا لَمۡ يَبۡصُرُواْ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضۡتُ قَبۡضَةٗ مِّنۡ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذۡتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتۡ لِي نَفۡسِي
transliteration: Qala basurtu bima lam yabsuroo bihi faqabadtu qabdatan min athari alrrasooli fanabathtuha wakathalika sawwalat lee nafsee
translation: He said, “I saw what they did not see. And I took a handful of traces from the messenger and flung it. And that is what my own self prompted me to do.”

arabic: قَالَ فَٱذۡهَبۡ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِي ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوۡعِدٗا لَّن تُخۡلَفَهُۥۖ وَٱنظُرۡ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِي ظَلۡتَ عَلَيۡهِ عَاكِفٗاۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِي ٱلۡيَمِّ نَسۡفًا
transliteration: Qala faithhab fainna laka fee alhayati an taqoola la misasa wainna laka mawAAidan lan tukhlafahu waonthur ila ilahika allathee thalta AAalayhi AAakifan lanuharriqannahu thumma lanansifannahu fee alyammi nasfan
translation: Moses said, “Begone! Indeed, it is ordained for you in this life to say, ‘Touch me not!’ And indeed there is a prediction for you that shall not fail. And look at your god that you hovered around! We will certainly burn it. Then we will certainly reduce it to dust and scatter it in the sea.”
notes:
It appears the Samiri was afflicted with a disease like leprosy.

Abode in Hell.

arabic: إِنَّمَآ إِلَٰهُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِي لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَۚ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَيۡءٍ عِلۡمٗا
transliteration: Innama ilahukumu Allahu allathee la ilaha illa huwa wasiAAa kulla shayin AAilman
translation: None but Allah is worthy of your worship! He knows everything.

arabic: كَذَٰلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَيۡكَ مِنۡ أَنۢبَآءِ مَا قَدۡ سَبَقَۚ وَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَٰكَ مِن لَّدُنَّا ذِكۡرٗا
transliteration: Kathalika naqussu AAalayka min anbai ma qad sabaqa waqad ataynaka min ladunna thikran
translation: Thus do We narrate to you some stories from the past. And We have verily given you the account (in the Qur’aan) from Ourselves.
notes: In other words, the stories narrated in the Qur’aan are all facts that have really happened, and not fictitious.

arabic: مَّنۡ أَعۡرَضَ عَنۡهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَحۡمِلُ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ وِزۡرًا
transliteration: Man aAArada AAanhu fainnahu yahmilu yawma alqiyamati wizran
translation: One who turns oneself away there from shall indeed bear a burden on the Day of Resurrection.
notes: From the account (the Qur’aan) mentioned in the last Verse.

arabic: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهِۖ وَسَآءَ لَهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ حِمۡلٗا
transliteration: Khalideena feehi wasaa lahum yawma alqiyamati himlan
translation: Burdened they shall remain. And too bad for them the burden, On the Day of Resurrection!

arabic: يَوۡمَ يُنفَخُ فِي ٱلصُّورِۚ وَنَحۡشُرُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ يَوۡمَئِذٖ زُرۡقٗا
transliteration: Yawma yunfakhu fee alssoori wanahshuru almujrimeena yawmaithin zurqan
translation: The Day, the trumpet is blown – the Day, We gather the sinful, blue with fear.

arabic: يَتَخَٰفَتُونَ بَيۡنَهُمۡ إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا عَشۡرٗا
transliteration: Yatakhafatoona baynahum in labithtum illa AAashran
translation: Whispering among them, “You stayed but ten days.”

arabic: نَّحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذۡ يَقُولُ أَمۡثَلُهُمۡ طَرِيقَةً إِن لَّبِثۡتُمۡ إِلَّا يَوۡمٗا
transliteration: Nahnu aAAlamu bima yaqooloona ith yaqoolu amthaluhum tareeqatan in labithtum illa yawman
translation: We know best what they say; and the one among them, with the exemplary method of giving estimates, would say, “You stayed but a day.”
notes: In Verse 23:113, the period so estimated is given as ‘a day or part of a day’. And in Verse 30:55, the estimate is ‘hardly an hour’. Those who had not taken care to follow their lives in accordance with the Qur’aan will thus be deluded to think that they had passed a very short time (hardly an hour to about 10 days) between their death and their resurrection. But those who followed the Qur’aan would know that they had passed a much longer time extending to thousands of years. The varying estimates of sinful people may well correspond to the varying times of their deaths prior to the Day of Resurrection. If a person died on the Last Day, when all worldly life will come to an end, his estimate would be ‘hardly an hour’, while it could be many years till the Day of Resurrection. And for a person dying in the initial period of human history, the estimate could be ten days, whereas the actual period would have been thousands of years.

arabic: وَيَسۡـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلۡجِبَالِ فَقُلۡ يَنسِفُهَا رَبِّي نَسۡفٗا
transliteration: Wayasaloonaka AAani aljibali faqul yansifuha rabbee nasfan
translation: And they ask you about the mountains. Say, “My Lord will pull them down to their roots.”

arabic: فَيَذَرُهَا قَاعٗا صَفۡصَفٗا
transliteration: Fayatharuha qaAAan safsafan
translation: And reduce it to a smooth, levelled plain.

arabic: لَّا تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجٗا وَلَآ أَمۡتٗا
transliteration: La tara feeha AAiwajan wala amtan
translation: You shall not see therein any ups and downs.

arabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلدَّاعِيَ لَا عِوَجَ لَهُۥۖ وَخَشَعَتِ ٱلۡأَصۡوَاتُ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ فَلَا تَسۡمَعُ إِلَّا هَمۡسٗا
transliteration: Yawmaithin yattabiAAoona alddaAAiya la AAiwaja lahu wakhashaAAati alaswatu lilrrahmani fala tasmaAAu illa hamsan
translation: That Day they shall follow the caller, and they shall have no alternative but to follow him. And the voices shall be low in deference to the Gracious One and you shall not hear but a soft murmur.

arabic: يَوۡمَئِذٖ لَّا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَٰعَةُ إِلَّا مَنۡ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ وَرَضِيَ لَهُۥ قَوۡلٗا
transliteration: Yawmaithin la tanfaAAu alshshafaAAatu illa man athina lahu alrrahmanu waradiya lahu qawlan
translation: That day no intercession shall benefit anyone except from him whom the Gracious One permits and whose word of intercession He approves.
notes: Please see study notes 482 and 483 on Verse 2.255 in this context.

arabic: يَعۡلَمُ مَا بَيۡنَ أَيۡدِيهِمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَهُمۡ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِهِۦ عِلۡمٗا
transliteration: YaAAlamu ma bayna aydeehim wama khalfahum wala yuheetoona bihi AAilman
translation: HE knows what they are aware of, and what they are not aware of, and what is beyond the sphere of their knowledge.

arabic: ۞وَعَنَتِ ٱلۡوُجُوهُ لِلۡحَيِّ ٱلۡقَيُّومِۖ وَقَدۡ خَابَ مَنۡ حَمَلَ ظُلۡمٗا
transliteration: WaAAanati alwujoohu lilhayyi alqayyoomi waqad khaba man hamala thulman
translation: And the faces shall be bent down before the Living, Everlasting One. And he who bears the burden of wrongdoing is certainly doomed.

arabic: وَمَن يَعۡمَلۡ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤۡمِنٞ فَلَا يَخَافُ ظُلۡمٗا وَلَا هَضۡمٗا
transliteration: Waman yaAAmal mina alssalihati wahuwa muminun fala yakhafu thulman wala hadman
translation: And he who does some good work and is also a believer, he shall have no fear of injustice or of usurpation.

arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُ قُرۡءَانًا عَرَبِيّٗا وَصَرَّفۡنَا فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلۡوَعِيدِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَتَّقُونَ أَوۡ يُحۡدِثُ لَهُمۡ ذِكۡرٗا
transliteration: Wakathalika anzalnahu quranan AAarabiyyan wasarrafna feehi mina alwaAAeedi laAAallahum yattaqoona aw yuhdithu lahum thikran
translation: And so have We sent it down as a Qur’aan in Arabic , and have detailed therein some of the things that are going to happen in the future so that they may fear Allah and take heed or that it may serve them as a Reminder of things that have gone by.
notes: In Verse 41:44, it has been explained that since the Prophet chosen was an Arab, the divine Message he was entrusted with had to be in his own tongue, Arabic.

arabic: فَتَعَٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡمَلِكُ ٱلۡحَقُّۗ وَلَا تَعۡجَلۡ بِٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مِن قَبۡلِ أَن يُقۡضَىٰٓ إِلَيۡكَ وَحۡيُهُۥۖ وَقُل رَّبِّ زِدۡنِي عِلۡمٗا
transliteration: FataAAala Allahu almaliku alhaqqu wala taAAjal bialqurani min qabli an yuqda ilayka wahyuhu waqul rabbi zidnee AAilman
translation: And exalted is Allah, the true Sovereign! And make no haste with reciting the Qur’aan before its revelation to you is completed. And say, “O my Lord! Increase my knowledge.”
notes:
It may be that the Prophet (peace on him) used to get anxious that he should not forget what was revealed to him, and would start reciting the Verse/s before the revelation was complete; hence, this divine admonition. Elsewhere in the Qur’aan (Verses 75:16 to 75:19), we are informed that Allah assured him of His help in making him remember. But this admonition has significance for us too. Allah tells us here that we should not read the Qur’aan hurriedly, but deliberate on what we read and try our best to understand it.

Not to go near a specified tree in Paradise.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ عَهِدۡنَآ إِلَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ مِن قَبۡلُ فَنَسِيَ وَلَمۡ نَجِدۡ لَهُۥ عَزۡمٗا
transliteration: Walaqad AAahidna ila adama min qablu fanasiya walam najid lahu AAazman
translation: And We had verily given a commandment to Adam in the past, but he forgot! And We did not find in him the trait of resoluteness.

arabic: وَإِذۡ قُلۡنَا لِلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسۡجُدُواْ لِأٓدَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓاْ إِلَّآ إِبۡلِيسَ أَبَىٰ
transliteration: Waith qulna lilmalaikati osjudoo liadama fasajadoo illa ibleesa aba
translation: And when We asked the angels to prostrate before Adam, prostrated they all except for Iblees. He (Iblees) refused!
notes: The divine words of this Verse are exactly like a part of Verse 2:34. Please see study notes 27 to 29 on that Verse of Chapter 2.

arabic: فَقُلۡنَا يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ إِنَّ هَٰذَا عَدُوّٞ لَّكَ وَلِزَوۡجِكَ فَلَا يُخۡرِجَنَّكُمَا مِنَ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ فَتَشۡقَىٰٓ
transliteration: Faqulna ya adamu inna hatha AAaduwwun laka walizawjika fala yukhrijannakuma mina aljannati fatashqa
translation: And We said, “O Adam! This is an enemy to you and to your wife; so, let him not drive you both out from the garden and thus make you miserable.”

arabic: إِنَّ لَكَ أَلَّا تَجُوعَ فِيهَا وَلَا تَعۡرَىٰ
transliteration: Inna laka alla tajooAAa feeha wala taAAra
translation: “It is indeed ordained for you that you shall not be hungry therein nor be naked.”

arabic: وَأَنَّكَ لَا تَظۡمَؤُاْ فِيهَا وَلَا تَضۡحَىٰ
transliteration: Waannaka la tathmao feeha wala tadha
translation: “And that you shall not be thirsty therein nor shall you feel hot.”

arabic: فَوَسۡوَسَ إِلَيۡهِ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ قَالَ يَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ هَلۡ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ ٱلۡخُلۡدِ وَمُلۡكٖ لَّا يَبۡلَىٰ
transliteration: Fawaswasa ilayhi alshshaytanu qala ya adamu hal adulluka AAala shajarati alkhuldi wamulkin la yabla
translation: Then the Satan seduced him, saying, “O Adam! Shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that shall not decay?”

arabic: فَأَكَلَا مِنۡهَا فَبَدَتۡ لَهُمَا سَوۡءَٰتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخۡصِفَانِ عَلَيۡهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلۡجَنَّةِۚ وَعَصَىٰٓ ءَادَمُ رَبَّهُۥ فَغَوَىٰ
transliteration: Faakala minha fabadat lahuma sawatuhuma watafiqa yakhsifani AAalayhima min waraqi aljannati waAAasa adamu rabbahu faghawa
translation: And they both succumbed to the satanic seduction, and then their sexuality got opened to them, and they both began to cover themselves with leaves of the garden. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and thus deviated from the right path.

arabic: ثُمَّ ٱجۡتَبَٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَتَابَ عَلَيۡهِ وَهَدَىٰ
transliteration: Thumma ijtabahu rabbuhu fataba AAalayhi wahada
translation: Then his Lord chose him , so He accepted his repentance and guided him.
notes: The Lord chose Adam to fulfil His word to the angels that He would send His vicegerent to the earth [ Verse 2:30] .

arabic: قَالَ ٱهۡبِطَا مِنۡهَا جَمِيعَۢاۖ بَعۡضُكُمۡ لِبَعۡضٍ عَدُوّٞۖ فَإِمَّا يَأۡتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّي هُدٗى فَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشۡقَىٰ
transliteration: Qala ihbita minha jameeAAan baAAdukum libaAAdin AAaduwwun faimma yatiyannakum minnee hudan famani ittabaAAa hudaya fala yadillu wala yashqa
translation: Allah said, “Down you both (Adam & Satan) go, together, in reciprocal enmity. And whenever Guidance comes to you from Me, then all those who follow My Guidance shall not go astray or be miserable.”

arabic: وَمَنۡ أَعۡرَضَ عَن ذِكۡرِي فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةٗ ضَنكٗا وَنَحۡشُرُهُۥ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ أَعۡمَىٰ
transliteration: Waman aAArada AAan thikree fainna lahu maAAeeshatan dankan wanahshuruhu yawma alqiyamati aAAman
translation: “And he who turns away from My Reminder shall lead a hard life, and We will raise him on the Day of Resurrection, blind.”
notes: Life of a person who fails to abide by the divine instructions given in the Qur’aan would be straitened, miserable, ignominious, humiliating, or otherwise unsatisfactory. He/she is bound to have a distressing feeling of unfulfilment, guilt and failure at the end of it. And it is a sad reflection on Muslims – on an overwhelming majority of them at the least – that they have woefully taken no heed of this divine mantra, enunciated in these two Verses 123 and 124, for leading a good, satisfactory life in this world.

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرۡتَنِيٓ أَعۡمَىٰ وَقَدۡ كُنتُ بَصِيرٗا
transliteration: Qala rabbi lima hashartanee aAAma waqad kuntu baseeran
translation: He will say, “My Lord! Why have You raised me blind? And I was certainly one able to see!”

arabic: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ أَتَتۡكَ ءَايَٰتُنَا فَنَسِيتَهَاۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ تُنسَىٰ
transliteration: Qala kathalika atatka ayatuna fanaseetaha wakathalika alyawma tunsa
translation: Allah will say, “Just as Our Verses/signs came to you, but you neglected them, so shall you be neglected this day.”

arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي مَنۡ أَسۡرَفَ وَلَمۡ يُؤۡمِنۢ بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِۦۚ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ أَشَدُّ وَأَبۡقَىٰٓ
transliteration: Wakathalika najzee man asrafa walam yumin biayati rabbihi walaAAathabu alakhirati ashaddu waabqa
translation: And thus, do We recompense him who is negligent and believes not in the Verses/signs of his Lord. And certainly, the punishment in the Hereafter is severe and abiding.

arabic: أَفَلَمۡ يَهۡدِ لَهُمۡ كَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا قَبۡلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡقُرُونِ يَمۡشُونَ فِي مَسَٰكِنِهِمۡۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأٓيَٰتٖ لِّأُوْلِي ٱلنُّهَىٰ
transliteration: Afalam yahdi lahum kam ahlakna qablahum mina alqurooni yamshoona fee masakinihim inna fee thalika laayatin liolee alnnuha
translation: Do they not take a lesson from the fact that We had destroyed many a generation before theirs, in whose dwelling-places they now move about? Indeed, there are signs in that for those who are prudent.

arabic: وَلَوۡلَا كَلِمَةٞ سَبَقَتۡ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَكَانَ لِزَامٗا وَأَجَلٞ مُّسَمّٗى
transliteration: Walawla kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika lakana lizaman waajalun musamman
translation: And had it not been that the Word had already gone forth from your Lord and an appointed term fixed, the same fate (of destruction in this world) that the earlier generations met with, would certainly be theirs (the generation addressed to in this Verse) too.

arabic: فَٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِ رَبِّكَ قَبۡلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمۡسِ وَقَبۡلَ غُرُوبِهَاۖ وَمِنۡ ءَانَآيِٕ ٱلَّيۡلِ فَسَبِّحۡ وَأَطۡرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرۡضَىٰ
transliteration: Faisbir AAala ma yaqooloona wasabbih bihamdi rabbika qabla tulooAAi alshshamsi waqabla ghuroobiha wamin anai allayli fasabbih waatrafa alnnahari laAAallaka tarda
translation: Exercise patience then over what they say. And glorify your Lord with His praise before the rising of the sun and before its going down. And glorify Him, during the night and during parts of the day, so that you get peace of mind.
notes:
The backdrop for this Verse is apparent from the context. The polytheists in Makkah would not embrace Islam despite the Prophet’s best efforts. And, in the divine scheme of things, those polytheists were not scheduled to be punished as yet. So they continued belittling the Prophet’s Mission. It was in these circumstances that Allah advises him to have patience and to seek his inner peace of mind by glorifying Allah continually, day and night.

But it would not be proper to interpret this Verse – as many commentators do – as prescribing the times for the ritual prayers. The Qur’aan, the Creator’s Own Manual for guidance of mankind, could not have been so vague in the timings. The timings are exactly prescribed in Verses 17:78, 11:114 and 2:238.

arabic: وَلَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيۡنَيۡكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعۡنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٗا مِّنۡهُمۡ زَهۡرَةَ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَا لِنَفۡتِنَهُمۡ فِيهِۚ وَرِزۡقُ رَبِّكَ خَيۡرٞ وَأَبۡقَىٰ
transliteration: Wala tamuddanna AAaynayka ila ma mattaAAna bihi azwajan minhum zahrata alhayati alddunya linaftinahum feehi warizqu rabbika khayrun waabqa
translation: And covet not that – which We have provided some couples among them with – of the glitter of this worldly life, whereby We try them. And what the Lord provides you with is better and lasting.

arabic: وَأۡمُرۡ أَهۡلَكَ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱصۡطَبِرۡ عَلَيۡهَاۖ لَا نَسۡـَٔلُكَ رِزۡقٗاۖ نَّحۡنُ نَرۡزُقُكَۗ وَٱلۡعَٰقِبَةُ لِلتَّقۡوَىٰ
transliteration: Wamur ahlaka bialssalati waistabir AAalayha la nasaluka rizqan nahnu narzuquka waalAAaqibatu lilttaqwa
translation: And enjoin prayer on your followers and be regular and steadfast thereon. We do not ask you for any provision; We give it to you. And in the end, it is those who fear Allah, and abide by His instructions, that get all the good things to enjoy.

arabic: وَقَالُواْ لَوۡلَا يَأۡتِينَا بِـَٔايَةٖ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦٓۚ أَوَلَمۡ تَأۡتِهِم بَيِّنَةُ مَا فِي ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلۡأُولَىٰ
transliteration: Waqaloo lawla yateena biayatin min rabbihi awalam tatihim bayyinatu ma fee alssuhufi aloola
translation: And they say, “Why does he not bring to us a sign from his Lord?” Has not there come to them a clear exposition of what the earlier scriptures contain?
notes: The Qur’aan reiterates and further explains and clarifies what the earlier divine scriptures mention or stipulate. This by itself is a clear sign from the Lord that the unlettered Muhammad (peace on him) was indeed a Messenger from Allah. How else could he know the accurate details?

arabic: وَلَوۡ أَنَّآ أَهۡلَكۡنَٰهُم بِعَذَابٖ مِّن قَبۡلِهِۦ لَقَالُواْ رَبَّنَا لَوۡلَآ أَرۡسَلۡتَ إِلَيۡنَا رَسُولٗا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَٰتِكَ مِن قَبۡلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخۡزَىٰ
transliteration: Walaw anna ahlaknahum biAAathabin min qablihi laqaloo rabbana lawla arsalta ilayna rasoolan fanattabiAAa ayatika min qabli an nathilla wanakhza
translation: And had We destroyed them with punishment before this, they would certainly have said, “O our Lord! Why did You not send us a Messenger? For, we should then have followed Your Verses/signs before we met disgrace and shame.”

arabic: قُلۡ كُلّٞ مُّتَرَبِّصٞ فَتَرَبَّصُواْۖ فَسَتَعۡلَمُونَ مَنۡ أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ ٱلسَّوِيِّ وَمَنِ ٱهۡتَدَىٰ
transliteration: Qul kullun mutarabbisun fatarabbasoo fasataAAlamoona man ashabu alssirati alssawiyyi wamani ihtada
translation: Say, “Everyone is waiting, so you too wait! You will soon come to know who is following the right path and who is guided.”
